summary: Iteration 29 'Ask anything' LLM-backed search tested end-to-end. Backend endpoint /api/search/ask works across all 6 intent types plus page/unknown fallbacks with correct JSON shape (q, intent, filters, answer, link, rows[title+sub], count, followups). Auth guards (401/403 + 400 on empty) all correct. Frontend palette opens, Ask toggle flips placeholder, 5 example queries render, clicking an example fires /api/search/ask and renders the Ask panel with answer card, Open → CTA, rows and followup chips, and the 'Switch to lookup' (ask-disable) link reverts to classic search.

issues: Multiple recharts ResponsiveContainer width(-1)/height(-1) warnings logged — cosmetic only, not caused by iter29 code, but could mask real issues in console.

action_items: No action required for iter29 — all 12 backend tests pass and the palette Ask flow is working end-to-end.
(Pre-existing, low priority) Recharts 'width(-1)/height(-1)' warnings on Overview page — investigate ResponsiveContainer parent sizing when convenient.
critical_code_review_comments: ask.py _INTENT_CACHE uses `key = question.lower()` globally (process-wide, not per-session). Spec says 'same q within a session'; current impl is broader (all users share cache). OK for correctness but worth documenting — a future enhancement could scope by user_id if answers ever become personalised.
ask.py _fetch_customers uses datetime.utcnow().date() (deprecated in 3.12+) where the rest of server.py uses datetime.now(timezone.utc). Low priority, will emit DeprecationWarning.

ask.py pricing/stock-aging fetchers filter by min units_sold (>=10) — rows with fewer sales silently dropped even if the user asked for them. Consider exposing a 'min_units' filter or lowering threshold to 3 for more inclusive results.
ask.py routes are under /api/search but imported late; good that include_router(ask_router) is after search_router so it doesn't clobber the classic lookup.

ask.py has no length guard on followups list — static, fine. But the 'unknown' intent returns followups key missing (only other intents get it) — confirmed in code: fallback branches return without followups key, meaning the frontend must handle its absence. Tests confirmed FE doesn't crash.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all critical flows pass; auto-enable heuristic flow blocked by a test-script issue, not an app bug)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 29 'Ask anything' is green. Tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_ask_iter29.py (12 cases, ~33s). LLM is Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations — first-hit latency 2-10s, cache hits <500ms. The palette's classic lookup mode still works alongside Ask. For UI: use data-testid selectors open-global-search, global-search-ask-toggle, global-search-ask-panel, ask-example-*, ask-row-N, ask-open-page, ask-followup-*, ask-disable. When the user's query matches no data (e.g. 'stores with stuck stock' in a single-day window), the panel still renders with 'count: 0' and no rows — that's correct behaviour (validated in screenshots).
